fields_json: {"name":"Greg Howard, DPT","bio":"Pediatric physical therapist at Kestrel Healthcare, Columbus OH. Former D1 hurdler turned PT. Creating family-friendly home exercise guides for children with developmental delays.","twitter":"@greghowardpt"}
{"name":"Sarah Mitchell, DPT","bio":"Pediatric physical therapist at Kestrel Healthcare. Co-reviewer of the Little Movers Guide chapters.","twitter":""}
{"title":"Gross Motor","slug":"gross-motor"}
{"title":"Fine Motor","slug":"fine-motor"}
{"title":"Core Strength","slug":"core-strength"}
{"title":"Torticollis","slug":"torticollis"}
{"title":"Balance & Coordination","slug":"balance"}
{"title":"Sensory Integration","slug":"sensory"}

{"title":"Prone Tummy Time - Beginner","slug":"prone-tummy-time-beginner","body":"Place infant on a firm flat surface on their tummy for 1-2 minutes, 3-4 times daily. Engage with a toy at eye level. Stop if infant shows distress.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-ch1-hero"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Tummy Time on Caregiver Chest","slug":"tummy-time-on-caregiver-chest","body":"Lie in a reclined position and place infant chest-to-chest. Caregiver's skin-to-skin contact and heartbeat provide calming input. Great starting point for resistant infants.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-ch1-hero"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Head-Turn Stretch - Torticollis","slug":"head-turn-stretch-torticollis","body":"With infant supine, gently rotate head to the restricted side and hold 30 seconds. Perform 3 reps each session, 3 sessions daily. Always consult your PT before starting.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-ex-torticollis-diagram"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Rolling Back to Tummy - Segmental","slug":"rolling-back-to-tummy-segmental","body":"Guide infant through a segmental roll: initiate with head, then shoulders, then hips. Use a favorite toy on the side to motivate the movement. 5-10 repetitions each direction.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-rolling-diagram"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Prop Sitting with Arm Support","slug":"prop-sitting-with-arm-support","body":"Place child in sitting and guide arms to straight, weight-bearing position on floor. Hold 10-15 seconds. Progress by moving a toy slightly out of reach to challenge trunk.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-ch3-hero"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Peg Board Fine Motor","slug":"peg-board-fine-motor","body":"Provide a large-hole peg board with chunky pegs. Encourage pincer grasp placement. Start with 5-10 pegs and gradually increase. Verbal praise after each placement.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-sarah-mitchell"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-fine-motor-pegs"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Single-Leg Stand - Balance Ages 2-4","slug":"single-leg-stand-balance","body":"Stand next to child near a wall. Ask child to pick up one foot and hold. Offer light finger support as needed. Goal: 3 seconds each leg, progressing to 5-10 seconds unassisted.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-ch5-hero"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Sensory Bin Tactile Exploration","slug":"sensory-bin-tactile-exploration","body":"Fill a shallow bin with rice, dried beans, or kinetic sand. Allow child to explore freely with hands and small scoops. Gradually introduce textures child finds aversive - never force.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-sensory-bin-photo"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Jumping - Two-Foot Takeoff Landing","slug":"jumping-two-foot-takeoff-landing","body":"Draft - landing mechanics cues to be added after Sarah review.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"published":false}

{"title":"Crawling Over Obstacles - Coordination","slug":"crawling-over-obstacles-coordination","body":"Create an obstacle course of pillows, foam rolls, and couch cushions. Guide child to creep over each barrier. Builds cross-body coordination and proprioceptive awareness.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-ch2-hero"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Beam Walking - Low Surface","slug":"beam-walking-low-surface","body":"Place a strip of masking tape on the floor or use a commercial balance beam no more than 1 inch high. Walk forward and backward with hand hold, progressing to independent walking.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-sarah-mitchell"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-balance-diagram"}},"published":true}

{"title":"Chin Tuck and Lateral Neck Stretch","slug":"chin-tuck-lateral-neck-stretch","body":"Support infant's head in midline with a gentle chin tuck, then slowly tilt to the non-affected side to elongate the tight sternocleidomastoid. Hold 30 seconds, 3 repetitions per session.","author":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Entry","id":"author-greg-howard"}},"heroImage":{"sys":{"type":"Link","linkType":"Asset","id":"asset-ex-torticollis-diagram"}},"published":true}
